deep_dive.title: KVEraser: Learning to Steer KV Cache for Efficient Localized Context Erasing
deep_dive.abstract: Post-hoc context erasing over the KV cache is challenging because a local edit has a global consequence: once a span has been processed, its influence propagates into the cached states of all subsequent tokens. This issue arises naturally in long-context LLM applications, where stale retrieved facts, incorrect tool observations, retracted user preferences, or harmful prompt injections may be identified only after prefill. Exact erasing must then recompute all tokens after the deleted span, making its computational cost depend on suffix length rather than erased-span length. We introduce KVEraser, a learned KV-cache editing method for efficient localized context erasing. Given a processed context and a span to remove, KVEraser replaces only the KV states of the erased interval with learned steering states while reusing the remaining cache unchanged. To learn a transferable erasing mechanism, we build a two-stage training pipeline: generic span-neighbor pre-training teaches the eraser to suppress the influence of the erased span, while task-specific fine-tuning adapts this capability to downstream scenarios. Experiments show that KVEraser nearly matches full recomputation in post-erasure performance on in-domain tasks across 1K--32K context lengths, while its latency increases by only 24% compared with a 17.6x increase for full recomputation. KVEraser also generalizes to unseen long-document QA tasks with harmful factual distractors, achieving the best performance among approximate baselines with a 3--4x speedup over full recomputation.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: KV-cache editing for localized context erasing, directly applicable to model editing for safety
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: KV缓存编辑用于局部上下文擦除，直接适用于模型编辑以提升安全性
deep_dive.llm_evidence: KV缓存编辑用于局部上下文擦除，直接适用于模型编辑以提升安全性
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: KV缓存编辑用于局部上下文擦除，直接适用于模型编辑以提升安全性
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: KVEraser learns to edit KV cache for efficient localized context erasing, enabling removal of harmful content without full recomputation.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 大语言模型在处理长上下文时，局部编辑会引发全局影响，传统精确擦除需要重算后续所有token，计算代价高昂。本文提出KVEraser，一种通过学习来编辑KV缓存的方法，仅替换待删除区间及其影响范围内的KV状态，实现高效的局部上下文擦除。实验表明，该方法在多个长上下文任务上接近精确重算的效果，同时显著降低延迟，为模型编辑提供了实用且可扩展的推理时干预手段。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 大语言模型在处理长上下文时，局部编辑会引发全局影响，传统精确擦除需要重算后续所有token，计算代价高昂。本文提出KVEraser，一种通过学习来编辑KV缓存的方法，仅替换待删除区间及其影响范围内的KV状态，实现高效的局部上下文擦除。实验表明，该方法在多个长上下文任务上接近精确重算的效果，同时显著降低延迟，为模型编辑提供了实用且可扩展的推理时干预手段。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: Find papers about model editing for safety in large language models

quick_skim.title: Adaptive and Explicit safe: Triggering Latent Safety Awareness in Large Reasoning Models
quick_skim.abstract: While Large Reasoning Models (LRMs) excel at complex tasks, they remain highly vulnerable to sophisticated jailbreaks and direct harmful queries. To address this vulnerability, prior works depend heavily on external manual data annotation for safety alignment. However, we observe that LRMs can inherently identify safety risks when being re-presented with original queries alongside their own reasoning trajectories -- a capability we term Latent Safety Awareness. To leverage this safety awareness, we first employ Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT) to explicitly induce safe tags to trigger safety analysis and guidance following the initial reasoning content for unsafe queries, while preserving standard responses for general queries to ensure adaptive triggering. Subsequently, we apply Direct Preference Optimization (DPO) to further enhance the correctness and stability of the safety analysis and guidance. Notably, responses required for both training stages are entirely generated by models being optimized. With (Safe Trigger) SFT and DPO, experimental results demonstrate significant safety enhancement. For example, the Attack Success Rate (ASR) of DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Llama-8B, on average, drops 24.65% and 36.72% on harmful and jailbreak benchmarks, respectively. Finally, our Safe Trigger method exerts almost no negative impact on general performance or user experience.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: triggers latent safety awareness via SFT for safety alignment
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 通过SFT触发潜在安全意识实现安全对齐
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 通过SFT触发潜在安全意识实现安全对齐
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 通过SFT触发潜在安全意识实现安全对齐
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes Adaptive and Explicit safe method that uses SFT to induce safe tags in reasoning models, activating latent safety awareness to defend against jailbreaks without external annotation.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文指出大型推理模型尽管能力强，仍易受越狱攻击。作者发现模型在重新看到原始查询和自身推理轨迹时能内在识别安全风险，称为潜在安全意识。据此提出自适应显式安全方法，通过有监督微调在推理内容后触发安全标签，从而激活安全意识并生成安全响应。实验表明该方法有效提升模型安全性，且无需大量外部标注。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该论文指出大型推理模型尽管能力强，仍易受越狱攻击。作者发现模型在重新看到原始查询和自身推理轨迹时能内在识别安全风险，称为潜在安全意识。据此提出自适应显式安全方法，通过有监督微调在推理内容后触发安全标签，从而激活安全意识并生成安全响应。实验表明该方法有效提升模型安全性，且无需大量外部标注。
